title: `make_folds` empirical refusal cliff as first-class error
source: [phase5/audit_null_distribution.md](phase5/audit_null_distribution.md) §B3 — empirical refusal boundary established at `n_events ≥ n_folds × 100 + train_min` (≥600 at defaults).
why: Today `make_folds` raises a `ValueError` whose message doesn't name the cliff. Phase 5's H4 extension hit this and the diagnostic required reading the matrix to understand why. Converting the ValueError into a structured error that *names the cliff* + suggests the parameter axis to relax (lower n_folds OR lower train_min OR get more events) makes the failure mode legible without changing the failure-handling contract (still raises; orchestrator still stops). **Critically NOT what B3 v3.2 will do** (that's an audit-class question, see B0007); this is a pipeline-level UX fix that's safe to land independently.
value_rationale: L (debugging clarity only; not a behavioral improvement)
scope: in [pipeline/walk_forward.py](pipeline/walk_forward.py) `make_folds`, replace the generic ValueError with `WalkForwardGeometryError(ValueError)` carrying structured fields `(events, required, n_folds, train_min)`. **Strictly IS-A ValueError** so existing `except ValueError:` handlers in [scripts/run_xau_d1.py](scripts/run_xau_d1.py) and [phase5/run_proposal.py](phase5/run_proposal.py) continue working unchanged; new callers wanting structured info can `isinstance`-check. Audit existing call sites to confirm. Test in `tests/test_walk_forward.py`. No behavioral change — failure-handling contract preserved.

history.reason: make_folds refusal cliff now raises WalkForwardGeometryError(ValueError) with structured fields (events, n_folds, train_min, test_pool_needed, required_events, shortfall) and a message naming the cliff + the 3 relaxable axes. Strictly IS-A ValueError so existing except-ValueError handlers in run_xau_d1/run_proposal are unchanged (call site propagates to subprocess as before; stderr now legible). Tests in tests/test_walk_forward.py: updated existing rejection test to match new message + added structured-error assertions + inclusive-boundary success. 572/572 pass.
feat(B0013): structured WalkForwardGeometryError names the refusal cliff
